description: 50 base scenarios derived from NAfME National Core Arts Standards and ABRSM grade descriptors. Replaces v1 placeholder data. Used as seeds for the two-LLM synthetic data generation pipeline (Opus=tutor, Sonnet=student). Distribution: Piano 15, Violin 12, Guitar 12, Voice 8, Drums 3.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.Ka - With guidance, demonstrate awareness of music concepts while engaging in music learning activities
scenarios.description: Young student is learning to find Middle C and the surrounding notes on the staff. They can identify the note by name when pointed to but cannot yet locate it independently on the keyboard.
scenarios.student_opener: I can find Middle C when my teacher shows me but I always forget where it is when I try by myself.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.1a - Demonstrate and explain, with teacher guidance, awareness of basic music concepts while reading music notation
scenarios.description: Student is learning quarter notes and half notes. They can clap rhythms in isolation but struggle to play and count at the same time.
scenarios.student_opener: I can clap the rhythm fine but when I try to play it on the piano my counting gets all messed up.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.2a - Apply established practice strategies and skills to address performance challenges
scenarios.description: Student is working on their first two-hand piece. Right hand is solid but left hand keeps losing its place when they try to play together.
scenarios.student_opener: My right hand knows the song perfectly but the second I add my left hand it all falls apart.

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 1 - Play pieces with correct notes, rhythm, and basic dynamics at a steady pulse
scenarios.description: Student is preparing their first ABRSM Grade 1 piece. Notes are mostly correct but tempo rushes in the difficult bars and slows down in the easy ones.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher says my tempo is uneven. I don't even notice I'm rushing until I listen back to a recording.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.4a - Demonstrate understanding of the structure and the elements of music in music selected for performance
scenarios.description: Student is working on a piece in C Major and starting to learn about key signatures. They understand C Major but do not yet understand why key signatures exist.
scenarios.student_opener: Why can't all songs just be in C Major? Why do we need key signatures at all?

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.4a - Apply teacher-provided and collaboratively developed criteria and feedback to evaluate accuracy of ensemble and individual performance
scenarios.description: Student has learned all the notes of a piece but is playing it mechanically without dynamic contrast. Teacher has marked piano and forte in the score but student ignores them.
scenarios.student_opener: I learned all the notes. Why does my teacher keep saying it doesn't sound like music yet?

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 2 - Demonstrate legato and staccato articulation within pieces; observe repeat marks and da capo
scenarios.description: Student is working on legato phrasing. They can play legato in slow practice but their default touch reverts to a slightly detached sound at performance tempo.
scenarios.student_opener: I can play it smooth when I go slowly but when I speed it up it sounds choppy again.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cr1.1.4a - Improvise rhythmic, melodic, and harmonic ideas, and explain connection to specific music concepts
scenarios.description: Student wants to start improvising but does not know where to begin. They know a pentatonic scale but freeze when asked to play freely.
scenarios.student_opener: I want to make up my own music but whenever I try I just play random notes that sound bad.

scenarios.standard: MU:Re7.1.5a - Demonstrate and explain how personal interests and musical understanding inform personal music selection for specific purposes
scenarios.description: Student is working on voicing — bringing out the melody in the right hand while keeping the accompaniment in the left hand softer. They understand the concept intellectually but both hands play at the same volume.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher told me to make the melody sing out but I don't know how to make one hand louder than the other.

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 3 - Demonstrate understanding of phrase shaping, balance between hands, and basic pedaling
scenarios.description: Student is learning to use the sustain pedal for the first time. They understand the concept but pedal at the wrong times, creating harmonic muddle.
scenarios.student_opener: I tried using the pedal like my teacher showed me but it just makes everything sound blurry.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.1.5a - Demonstrate and explain how the selection of music to perform is influenced by personal interest, knowledge, purpose, and context
scenarios.description: Student is preparing a performance piece and wants to add personal interpretation but is afraid of deviating from what is written. They play the notes and rhythms correctly but without character.
scenarios.student_opener: How do I add my own expression to a piece without playing it wrong?

scenarios.standard: MU:Cn10.0.6a - Demonstrate how interests, knowledge, and skills relate to personal choices and intent when creating, performing, and responding to music
scenarios.description: Student is working on sight-reading. They can read notes they know but lock up when encountering new rhythmic patterns or ledger lines.
scenarios.student_opener: Every time I try to sight-read something new I have to stop and figure out every single note. It takes forever.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.3a - Demonstrate understanding of expressive qualities and how creators use them to convey expressive intent
scenarios.description: Student is learning about forte and piano but applies them randomly rather than in response to musical context or written markings.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher put f and p in my music but I can't remember when I'm supposed to be loud or quiet while I'm playing.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr6.1.Ka - With guidance, perform music with expression, technical accuracy, and appropriate interpretation
scenarios.description: A young student has just learned their first short piece and is excited to play it. They rush through it when excited and play very quietly from nervousness.
scenarios.student_opener: I learned the whole song! Do you want to hear it? I practiced it all week!

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 4-5 - Demonstrate facility with varied articulation, pedaling, and stylistic awareness across different musical periods
scenarios.description: Student is working on a Baroque piece and a Romantic piece simultaneously. They play both in the same style and cannot differentiate the articulation conventions.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher gave me a Bach piece and a Chopin piece but I don't understand why I have to play them differently.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.Ka - With guidance, demonstrate awareness of music concepts while engaging in music learning activities
scenarios.description: Young student is learning how to hold the bow for the first time. They grip it tightly in their fist instead of using a relaxed curved finger placement.
scenarios.student_opener: Holding the bow is really hard. My fingers don't want to go in the right place.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.1a - With guidance, apply practice strategies to address performance challenges in music
scenarios.description: Student has started learning open string bowing. Their bow goes crooked rather than parallel to the bridge and they produce a scratchy tone.
scenarios.student_opener: My violin sounds really scratchy and squeaky when I play. How do I make it sound better?

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Initial - Play open strings and first simple pieces with basic bow control and left hand position
scenarios.description: Student is placing their first fingers on the string but presses too hard, causing pitch to go sharp and hand to tense up.
scenarios.student_opener: When I put my finger down the note sounds weird, like it's not the right pitch.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.2a - Demonstrate understanding of simple musical concepts while performing with technical accuracy
scenarios.description: Student is learning detache bowing and struggles to use the full bow length. They use only the middle third of the bow and produce a thin, short sound.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher says I need to use more bow but I don't understand what that means when I'm playing.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.3a - Apply teacher-provided criteria to evaluate personal performance and expression
scenarios.description: Student is working on intonation in first position. They can hear when a note is out of tune after they play it but cannot hear it in advance or self-correct in real time.
scenarios.student_opener: I can hear that I played the wrong note after I play it, but I don't know how to fix it before it comes out.

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 1 - Play pieces with correct intonation in first position; perform basic bowing patterns
scenarios.description: Student is working on their first slur. They understand the concept intellectually but bow speed changes awkwardly mid-slur and tone becomes uneven.
scenarios.student_opener: When I try to slur two notes my bow kind of stops and starts and it sounds bumpy.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cr2.1.4a - Demonstrate selected and developed musical ideas for improvisations, arrangements, or compositions to express intent
scenarios.description: Student is learning to shift to third position for the first time. They understand the target position but fear of going off-pitch makes them hesitate and the shift sounds clunky.
scenarios.student_opener: Shifting feels really scary. What if I miss the note?

scenarios.standard: MU:Re9.1.4a - Evaluate musical works and performances based on analysis and established criteria
scenarios.description: Student has been told to add vibrato to a lyrical piece. They have practiced the wrist motion in isolation but vibrato is tense and narrow when added to actual playing.
scenarios.student_opener: I can do the vibrato motion on its own but when I try to do it while playing it gets really tight and fast.

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 3 - Demonstrate varied bow strokes including spiccato; play in third position
scenarios.description: Student is attempting spiccato (bouncing bow stroke) but the bow bounces unevenly and randomly rather than in a controlled, rhythmic pattern.
scenarios.student_opener: I can't get spiccato to work. Sometimes the bow bounces fine and sometimes it just drags.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.1.6a - Demonstrate an understanding of the expressive intent of a selected piece based on analysis of the music
scenarios.description: Student can play the technical demands of a piece correctly but their teacher says it lacks musicality. Student does not know how to translate expressive markings into actual sound.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher writes more musical everywhere in my music but I don't know what that actually means or how to do it.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.2a - Demonstrate understanding of music concepts including pitch, rhythm, and dynamics while performing
scenarios.description: Student is learning to read rhythms from notation. They understand quarter and half notes individually but confuse them when reading a full line of music.
scenarios.student_opener: I know what a quarter note is and I know what a half note is but when I look at a whole line of music I lose track.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cn11.0.6a - Demonstrate understanding of relationships between music and the other arts, disciplines, and contexts of society
scenarios.description: Student is preparing a performance of a Romantic concerto excerpt and wants to understand how to convey the emotional content the composer intended.
scenarios.student_opener: My piece is from the Romantic era. What does that mean for how I should play it?

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.1a - Demonstrate awareness of basic music concepts while playing simple melodies
scenarios.description: Student has just started learning guitar. Their fretting hand fingertips hurt and they keep stopping mid-practice because of the discomfort.
scenarios.student_opener: My fingers really hurt when I press the strings. Is it supposed to hurt this much?

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.2a - Apply established practice strategies to address performance challenges
scenarios.description: Student is learning their first open position chord (G Major). They can form the chord shape slowly but struggle to get all strings ringing cleanly.
scenarios.student_opener: When I play my G chord some strings sound muted or buzzy. I can't figure out what I'm doing wrong.

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Initial/Grade 1 - Play simple melodies in first position with correct finger placement and basic right-hand technique
scenarios.description: Student is learning to pick individual strings in a simple melody but their pick accidentally hits adjacent strings, creating unwanted noise.
scenarios.student_opener: When I try to play one string at a time my pick keeps hitting the wrong strings.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.3a - Demonstrate understanding of expressive qualities and how they convey expressive intent
scenarios.description: Student is working on a simple song and wants to add strumming rather than single notes. They can strum downstrokes but cannot coordinate an up-down strumming pattern.
scenarios.student_opener: I can strum down fine but when I try to go up and down my hand gets confused and I lose the beat.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.4a - Apply criteria and feedback to evaluate performance challenges and refine technique
scenarios.description: Student has learned three open chords (G, C, D) individually but chord transitions between them are very slow and disrupt the rhythmic flow of songs.
scenarios.student_opener: I know all three chords but when I switch between them I have to stop the music and think about where to put my fingers.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cr1.1.4a - Improvise and develop rhythmic and melodic ideas connected to musical concepts
scenarios.description: Student is learning barre chords and cannot get the F Major barre chord to ring cleanly. They have enough strength but their index finger is not fully pressing all strings.
scenarios.student_opener: I cannot get the F chord. My fingers aren't strong enough or something. I've been trying for weeks.

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Grade 2 - Demonstrate facility with basic position chord playing, simple arpeggios, and first attempts at barre chords
scenarios.description: Student is learning fingerpicking patterns. They can pluck individual strings but struggle to maintain a steady pattern while also fretting chord shapes.
scenarios.student_opener: I can do the fingerpicking pattern on open strings but the second I add chords my right hand messes up the pattern.

scenarios.standard: MU:Re8.1.5a - Interpret and describe expressive intent and meaning in musical works by analyzing contextual clues
scenarios.description: Student knows the minor pentatonic scale and can play it up and down but sounds mechanical when improvising — just running up and down the scale with no phrasing.
scenarios.student_opener: I know the pentatonic scale but every time I solo I just run up and down it and it sounds boring.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.1.5a - Demonstrate how selection of music to perform is influenced by personal interests and musical understanding
scenarios.description: Student wants to learn to read tablature to learn songs from the internet but their teacher has been teaching standard notation. Student is frustrated by what they see as a disconnect.
scenarios.student_opener: Why do I need to learn sheet music? All the songs I want to learn online just have tabs.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cr3.1.6a - Evaluate and refine craftsmanship of musical works using feedback and criteria
scenarios.description: Student is preparing to perform a fingerstyle arrangement of a song they love. The piece is slightly above their current level and they are frustrated by slow progress.
scenarios.student_opener: I've been working on this song for three weeks and it still doesn't sound right. I'm starting to think I just can't do it.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr6.1.2a - Perform music for an audience using established notation, technique, and expression
scenarios.description: Student just learned their first complete song on guitar and is proud but nervous about playing it for family. They play perfectly when alone but freeze when someone watches.
scenarios.student_opener: I can play the song perfectly by myself but when my mom watches me I forget everything.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cn10.0.7a - Demonstrate how personal interests, knowledge, and skills inform personal choices when creating and performing music
scenarios.description: Student wants to understand music theory as it applies to guitar — specifically why certain chords sound good together and how to figure out which chords belong to a key.
scenarios.student_opener: I want to learn why some chords sound good together. How do I know what chords go with each other?

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.Ka - With guidance, demonstrate awareness of basic music concepts while engaging in singing activities
scenarios.description: Very young student is learning the concept of singing versus speaking. They frequently speak-sing instead of using their head voice, staying in chest voice throughout.
scenarios.student_opener: I don't know how to make my voice go up high. It's like my voice doesn't want to go there.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.1a - With guidance, apply practice strategies to address performance challenges in singing
scenarios.description: Student is learning that singing requires the whole body and is working on releasing tension. They sing with hunched shoulders and a locked jaw, affecting tone quality.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher says I look tense when I sing. I don't feel tense. How do I fix that?

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.2a - Demonstrate understanding of music concepts including breath, posture, and basic pitch matching
scenarios.description: Student is learning to sing a simple melody but runs out of breath before the phrase ends. They take a breath partway through and disrupt the musical line.
scenarios.student_opener: I always run out of air before the phrase is done. What am I doing wrong?

scenarios.standard: ABRSM Initial - Sing simple songs with basic pitch accuracy, clear diction, and controlled breathing
scenarios.description: Student is working on matching pitch. They can hear when they are off pitch after the fact but cannot adjust in real time while singing.
scenarios.student_opener: I know I'm going off pitch but I don't know how to fix it while I'm singing. It's like I can't hear myself.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.4a - Apply criteria and feedback to improve singing technique and expressive delivery
scenarios.description: Student is working on singing through a passaggio (register transition). They have an audible break between their chest voice and head voice.
scenarios.student_opener: My voice makes this weird cracking sound right in the middle of my range. How do I stop that from happening?

scenarios.standard: MU:Re8.1.4a - Demonstrate awareness of expressive qualities in vocal music and how they contribute to expressive intent
scenarios.description: Student can sing the notes and words of a song correctly but their delivery is flat and emotionless. Teacher says they need more expression but student does not know what to do differently.
scenarios.student_opener: I know all the words and the tune but my teacher says it doesn't sound like I feel anything when I sing it.

scenarios.standard: MU:Cr2.1.6a - Select and refine musical ideas to express intent in compositions and arrangements
scenarios.description: Student is working on diction in a classical art song. Certain vowel sounds cause their tone quality to change or become nasal.
scenarios.student_opener: Some vowels sound fine when I sing them but when I sing the vowel ee my voice gets really nasal and tight.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.1.5a - Demonstrate how choices in performance are informed by personal understanding of expressive intent
scenarios.description: Student is preparing a song for an audition and wants to add personal stylistic interpretation but is unsure how much to deviate from the written melody and rhythm.
scenarios.student_opener: I'm auditioning with this song and I want to make it my own but I don't know how much I'm allowed to change.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr4.2.1a - Demonstrate awareness of basic music concepts including pulse and rhythm while performing on percussion
scenarios.description: Young student is learning to maintain a steady beat on a practice pad. They rush when excited and drag when concentrating too hard.
scenarios.student_opener: My teacher says I'm not staying on the beat. Sometimes I go faster and sometimes I go slower but I can't feel it happening.

scenarios.standard: MU:Pr5.1.3a - Apply practice strategies to develop technical accuracy and consistency in rhythmic performance
scenarios.description: Student is learning their first basic drum beat with kick-snare-hi-hat coordination. They can play each limb independently but cannot coordinate all three simultaneously.
scenarios.student_opener: I can do the kick and hi-hat together, and I can do the snare and hi-hat together, but when I try to put them all together my brain shuts down.

scenarios.standard: MU:Re7.1.4a - Demonstrate and explain how the elements of music, including rhythm and form, are used in selected works
scenarios.description: Student has mastered a basic beat and wants to add fills between sections. Their fills always interrupt the groove and they land in the wrong place coming back into the beat.
scenarios.student_opener: Whenever I try to do a fill I lose track of where the beat is supposed to start back up.
